architecture_fit.note: model_fn just recomputes the ground-truth softmax(QK^T/√d) the generator uses; perfect numbers but circular — it is the computation, not an explanation of which QK interactions drive output.

baseline_comparison.note: The uniform linear_baseline (provided by the framework) is shown failing while the method scores ~1.0, but the attempt contributes no baseline of its own.

faithfulness.note: No model, ablation, or causal check; README acknowledges it is hand-built but does not propose what a faithfulness/ablation test would look like.

operating_range.note: Holds at ~1.0 across all four qk_alignment regimes (the goal's axis), but trivially so since it returns the ground truth — no stress where it degrades.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Mechanism is hand-written torch rather than learned, but it merely re-expresses the data generator, so it demonstrates little mechanistic understanding.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab is two static markdown lines with no chart/heatmap; the Benchmark dashboard function is never wired into `demo`, so the required two-tab view is absent.

visualisation_rationale.note: README argues a heatmap would be redundant and a text claim is clearer, but it justifies showing essentially nothing checkable.

notes: A first-pass ceiling that returns the exact ground-truth attention the generator uses, so all benchmark metrics are trivially ~1.0. It is a useful sanity reference but circular as a mechanistic explanation: no model delta from base_model.py, no faithfulness/causal check, and the Demo tab shows only static text with no real visualisation. Scores reflect a correct-but-trivial baseline that a real explanation method must improve upon.
